name: cowpatty
title: cowpatty
description:
Brute-force WPA dictionary attack
If you are auditing WPA-PSK or WPA2-PSK networks, you can use
this tool to identify weak passphrases that were used to generate the
PMK.  Supply a libpcap capture file that includes the 4-way handshake, a
dictionary file of passphrases to guess with, and the SSID for the
network.

arguments.description: Check for valid 4-way frames, does not crack
arguments.raw: -c  Check for valid 4-way frames, does not crack

arguments.description: Print this help information and exit
arguments.raw: -h  Print this help information and exit

arguments.description: Network SSID (enclose in quotes if SSID includes spaces)
arguments.raw: -s  Network SSID (enclose in quotes if SSID includes spaces)

arguments.description: Print verbose information (more -v for more verbosity)
arguments.raw: -v  Print verbose information (more -v for more verbosity)

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cowpatty because Brute-force WPA dictionary attack.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cowpatty as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cowpatty < -d | -r > < HASH-FILE | CAPTURE-FILE > -f WORDLIST -s SSID` as documented upstream for cowpatty.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cowpatty with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cowpatty --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
